entities.name: Disciplined restraint
entities.context: Core principle that optimal growth requires holding back rather than maximal effort

entities.name: Plateau of over-exertion
entities.context: Phenomenon where pushing to limits early prevents foundational conditioning for peak performance

entities.name: Non-linear adaptation curve
entities.context: Framework describing how growth and recovery drive structural change

entities.name: Recovery
entities.context: Identified as the primary driver of structural change in adaptation

entities.name: Heart-rate variability
entities.context: Example of cellular adaptation that can be masked by systemic fatigue

entities.name: Mitochondrial density
entities.context: Example of cellular adaptation affected by training intensity

entities.name: Consistency over intensity
entities.context: Strategic principle for founders and athletes to optimize performance

entities.name: Strategic under-performance
entities.context: Technique of intentionally performing below capacity short-term to enable elite long-term results

relationships.context: Both emphasize holding back effort to preserve adaptive capacity
Over-exertion creates systemic fatigue that undermines recovery-driven adaptation
Recovery is the primary driver within the non-linear adaptation framework
HRV is a marker of cellular adaptation enabled by proper recovery
Mitochondrial adaptation depends on recovery from training stress
Under-performance is the tactical implementation of the consistency principle
